Five witnesses were examined for
the state during the course of the
morning session, police secretary,
mayors secretary, two reporters
and a captain of detectives.


Is the trial a fair one? The au-
thoriues claim that it is. that they
are not "railroading the |. w. W.
leaders into prison. Yet this morn
ing Prosecuting Attorney Pueh was
upheld at every point by Justice
Stocker, while every objection of
Attorney Fred H. Moore, no mat
ter how trivial, was overruled by
Justice Stocker.


GuRLEY FLYNN's SPEECHES.


Following his opening address to
the jurors. in which he made Known
his intention of proving that the
defendant was guilty of conspiracy
in plotting to break a municipal
ordinance, be placed in evidence a
copy of portions Of speech pur-
ported to have been made by the
defendant in the |. ". w. hall and
taken down in short hand by leon
Hopper, secretary to Chief of Police
Sullivan.


In this address Gurley Flynn ap-
pealed for help, financially and to
fill the Jail, condemned the Spokes-
man-Review as an organ Oh the en-
ployment agents, and made gen
eral free speech" appeal.


John Lee. Mayor Pratt's secre-
tary, testified that he attended a
meeting of the l. W w. on Thurs
day evening, November II, at
which the girl orator addressed
her fellow workers, eXhorting
them 10 make several more dem
onstrations and the police would be
whipped. She declared that TIE
